published: European Data Protection Board Guidelines on biometric data processing under GDPR
Fundamental Rights Agency — AI and Fundamental Rights (structural alignment with Art. 5 prohibitions)
ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — AI management system (governance controls relevant to Art. 5 self-assessment)
upcoming: AI Office guidelines on interpretation of Art. 5 prohibited practices (expected 2026)
CEN-CENELEC guidance on prohibited practice identification methodologies

primary_official_source.name: EUR-Lex — Official Journal of the European Union
primary_official_source.eli_note: ELI (European Legislation Identifier) is designed for permanent persistence. Preferred over CELEX-based URLs.

obligations.source_quote: The following AI practices shall be prohibited: (a) the placing on the market, the putting into service or the use of an AI system that deploys subliminal techniques beyond a person's consciousness or purposefully manipulative or deceptive techniques, with the objective, or the effect of materially distorting the behaviour of a person or a group of persons by appreciably impairing their ability to make an informed decision, thereby causing them to take a decision that they would not have otherwise taken in a manner that causes or is reasonably likely to cause that person, another person or group of persons significant harm.
obligations.addressee_note: Applies to any natural or legal person placing on the market, putting into service, or using an AI system (provider, deployer, or any other party)

decomposed_atoms.description: AI systems must not deploy subliminal techniques beyond conscious perception
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT deploy subliminal techniques beyond a person's consciousness that materially distort behaviour and cause or are reasonably likely to cause significant harm

decomposed_atoms.description: AI systems must not deploy purposefully manipulative techniques
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT deploy purposefully manipulative techniques that appreciably impair informed decision-making and cause or are reasonably likely to cause significant harm

decomposed_atoms.description: AI systems must not deploy purposefully deceptive techniques
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT deploy purposefully deceptive techniques that appreciably impair informed decision-making and cause or are reasonably likely to cause significant harm

decomposed_atoms.description: The prohibition is triggered by significant harm or reasonable likelihood thereof
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition applies when the manipulative/subliminal/deceptive technique causes or is REASONABLY LIKELY to cause significant harm — actual harm is not required, likelihood suffices

obligations.source_quote: The following AI practices shall be prohibited: (b) the placing on the market, the putting into service or the use of an AI system that exploits any of the vulnerabilities of a natural person or a specific group of persons due to their age, disability or a specific social or economic situation, with the objective, or the effect, of materially distorting the behaviour of that person or a person belonging to that group in a manner that causes or is reasonably likely to cause that person or another person significant harm.
obligations.addressee_note: Applies to any natural or legal person placing on the market, putting into service, or using an AI system

decomposed_atoms.description: AI systems must not exploit age-related vulnerabilities (children, elderly)
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT exploit vulnerabilities due to age (e.g., targeting children or elderly) to materially distort behaviour causing significant harm

decomposed_atoms.description: AI systems must not exploit disability-related vulnerabilities
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT exploit vulnerabilities due to disability (cognitive, physical, sensory) to materially distort behaviour causing significant harm

decomposed_atoms.description: AI systems must not exploit social or economic vulnerability
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT exploit vulnerabilities due to specific social or economic situation (poverty, homelessness, immigration status) to materially distort behaviour causing significant harm

decomposed_atoms.description: Both material distortion of behaviour and significant harm must be present
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition is triggered when exploitation BOTH materially distorts behaviour AND causes or is reasonably likely to cause significant harm — both elements are required

obligations.source_quote: The following AI practices shall be prohibited: (c) the placing on the market, the putting into service or the use of AI systems for the evaluation or classification of natural persons or groups of persons over a certain period of time based on their social behaviour or known, inferred or predicted personal or personality characteristics, with the social score leading to either or both of the following: (i) detrimental or unfavourable treatment of certain natural persons or groups of persons in social contexts that are unrelated to the contexts in which the data was originally generated or collected; (ii) detrimental or unfavourable treatment of certain natural persons or groups of persons that is unjustified or disproportionate to their social behaviour or its gravity.
obligations.addressee_note: Applies to any natural or legal person — the final text removed the earlier draft limitation to public authorities; social scoring by private entities is also prohibited

decomposed_atoms.description: AI systems must not produce social scores leading to detrimental treatment in unrelated contexts
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT evaluate or classify persons based on social behaviour or personal characteristics where the resulting score leads to detrimental treatment in social contexts UNRELATED to where the data was originally generated

decomposed_atoms.description: AI systems must not produce social scores leading to disproportionate treatment
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT evaluate or classify persons based on social behaviour or personal characteristics where the resulting score leads to detrimental treatment that is UNJUSTIFIED or DISPROPORTIONATE to the behaviour or its gravity

decomposed_atoms.description: The prohibition covers scores based on known, inferred, or predicted characteristics
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition applies whether the social score is based on KNOWN, INFERRED, or PREDICTED personal or personality characteristics — all three bases are covered

decomposed_atoms.description: The prohibition covers scoring over a period of time
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition applies to evaluation or classification over a certain period of time — aggregate behavioural scoring, not single-instance assessments in isolation

obligations.source_quote: The following AI practices shall be prohibited: (d) the placing on the market, the putting into service for this specific purpose, or the use of an AI system for making risk assessments of natural persons in order to assess or predict the risk of a natural person committing a criminal offence, based solely on the profiling of a natural person or on assessing their personality traits and characteristics; this prohibition shall not apply to AI systems used to support the human assessment of the involvement of a person in a criminal activity, which is already based on objective and verifiable facts directly linked to a criminal activity.
obligations.addressee_note: Applies broadly, but primarily relevant to law enforcement and justice system actors

decomposed_atoms.description: AI systems must not assess criminal risk based solely on profiling
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT assess or predict the risk of a person committing a criminal offence based SOLELY on profiling of that person

decomposed_atoms.description: AI systems must not assess criminal risk based solely on personality traits
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT assess or predict criminal risk based solely on assessing a person's personality traits and characteristics — without objective factual basis

decomposed_atoms.description: Exception: AI may support human assessment when based on objective verifiable facts
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition does NOT apply when AI is used to SUPPORT (not replace) human assessment of involvement in criminal activity that is ALREADY based on objective, verifiable facts DIRECTLY linked to criminal activity

decomposed_atoms.description: The exception has three cumulative conditions
decomposed_atoms.requirement: For the exception to apply, ALL three conditions must be met: (1) the AI supports (not replaces) human assessment; (2) the assessment is based on objective, verifiable facts; (3) those facts are directly linked to criminal activity

cross_references.context: AI systems supporting criminal justice that are not prohibited under Art. 5(1)(d) may still be classified as high-risk under Art. 6

obligations.source_quote: The following AI practices shall be prohibited: (e) the placing on the market, the putting into service for this specific purpose, or the use of AI systems that create or expand facial recognition databases through the untargeted scraping of facial images from the internet or CCTV footage.
obligations.addressee_note: Applies to any natural or legal person — no exceptions exist for this prohibition

decomposed_atoms.description: Must not create facial recognition databases by scraping the internet
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT create or expand facial recognition databases through untargeted scraping of facial images from the internet

decomposed_atoms.description: Must not create facial recognition databases by scraping CCTV footage
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT create or expand facial recognition databases through untargeted scraping of facial images from CCTV footage

decomposed_atoms.description: The prohibition targets untargeted scraping specifically
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition applies to UNTARGETED scraping — bulk, indiscriminate collection. Targeted collection with lawful basis is not covered by this specific prohibition

decomposed_atoms.description: Both creating new databases and expanding existing ones are prohibited
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition covers both CREATING new facial recognition databases AND EXPANDING existing ones through untargeted scraping

obligations.source_quote: The following AI practices shall be prohibited: (f) the placing on the market, the putting into service for this specific purpose, or the use of AI systems to infer emotions of a natural person in the areas of workplace and education institutions, except where the use of the AI system is intended to be put in place or into the market for medical or safety reasons.
obligations.addressee_note: Applies to any natural or legal person deploying emotion recognition in workplace or education settings

decomposed_atoms.description: AI systems must not infer emotions in the workplace
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT be used to infer emotions of persons in the area of workplace — including employee mood detection, engagement monitoring, hiring interview emotion analysis

decomposed_atoms.description: AI systems must not infer emotions in educational institutions
decomposed_atoms.requirement: An AI system SHALL NOT be used to infer emotions of persons in the area of education institutions — including student attention monitoring, engagement tracking, and emotional state assessment

decomposed_atoms.description: Exception for medical purposes
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition does NOT apply when the AI system is intended for medical reasons — e.g., therapeutic monitoring, clinical assessment, mental health support. The medical purpose must be genuine and documented

decomposed_atoms.description: Exception for safety purposes
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition does NOT apply when the AI system is intended for safety reasons — e.g., driver drowsiness detection, operator fatigue monitoring in safety-critical operations. The safety purpose must be genuine and documented

obligations.source_quote: The following AI practices shall be prohibited: (g) the placing on the market, the putting into service for this specific purpose, or the use of biometric categorisation systems that categorise individually natural persons based on their biometric data to deduce or infer their race, political opinions, trade union membership, religious or philosophical beliefs, sex life or sexual orientation; this prohibition does not cover any labelling or filtering of lawfully acquired biometric datasets, such as images, based on biometric data or categorizing of biometric data in the area of law enforcement.
obligations.addressee_note: Applies to any natural or legal person, with narrow exceptions for dataset labelling and law enforcement

decomposed_atoms.description: Must not use biometric data to infer race
decomposed_atoms.requirement: A biometric categorization system SHALL NOT categorize persons based on biometric data to deduce or infer their race

decomposed_atoms.description: Must not use biometric data to infer political opinions
decomposed_atoms.requirement: A biometric categorization system SHALL NOT categorize persons based on biometric data to deduce or infer their political opinions

decomposed_atoms.description: Must not use biometric data to infer trade union membership
decomposed_atoms.requirement: A biometric categorization system SHALL NOT categorize persons based on biometric data to deduce or infer their trade union membership

decomposed_atoms.description: Must not use biometric data to infer religious or philosophical beliefs
decomposed_atoms.requirement: A biometric categorization system SHALL NOT categorize persons based on biometric data to deduce or infer their religious or philosophical beliefs

decomposed_atoms.description: Must not use biometric data to infer sex life or sexual orientation
decomposed_atoms.requirement: A biometric categorization system SHALL NOT categorize persons based on biometric data to deduce or infer their sex life or sexual orientation

decomposed_atoms.description: Exception for lawful labelling/filtering of biometric datasets
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition does NOT apply to labelling or filtering of lawfully acquired biometric datasets — e.g., annotating training data images based on biometric data, provided acquisition was lawful

decomposed_atoms.description: Exception for law enforcement biometric categorization
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition does NOT apply to categorizing of biometric data in the area of law enforcement — subject to applicable legal basis and safeguards

obligations.source_quote: The following AI practices shall be prohibited: (h) the use of 'real-time' remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for the purposes of law enforcement, unless and in so far as such use is strictly necessary for one of the following objectives: (i) the targeted search for specific victims of abduction, trafficking in human beings or sexual exploitation of human beings, as well as the search for missing persons; (ii) the prevention of a specific, substantial and imminent threat to the life or physical safety of natural persons or a genuine and present or genuine and foreseeable threat of a terrorist attack; (iii) the localisation or identification of a person suspected of having committed a criminal offence, for the purpose of conducting a criminal investigation or prosecution or executing a criminal penalty for offences referred to in Annex II and punishable in the Member State concerned by a custodial sentence or a detention order for a maximum period of at least four years.
obligations.addressee_note: This prohibition specifically targets law enforcement use in publicly accessible spaces. Non-law-enforcement real-time biometric ID in public spaces is regulated under Art. 6 as high-risk

decomposed_atoms.description: Real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces is prohibited for law enforcement
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The use of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces SHALL be prohibited for law enforcement purposes — this is the default rule

decomposed_atoms.description: Real-time means live or near-live processing without significant delay
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition covers 'real-time' systems — live biometric matching, not post-facto analysis. Post-facto analysis is regulated as high-risk under Art. 6 but not banned

decomposed_atoms.description: The prohibition applies only in publicly accessible spaces
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The prohibition applies in PUBLICLY ACCESSIBLE spaces — streets, parks, transport hubs, shopping centres. Private or access-controlled spaces are not covered by this specific prohibition

decomposed_atoms.description: Exception (i): targeted search for victims or missing persons
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Exception: real-time biometric ID MAY be used for targeted search for specific victims of abduction, trafficking, sexual exploitation, or search for missing persons — strictly necessary use only

decomposed_atoms.description: Exception (ii): prevention of imminent threats or terrorist attacks
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Exception: real-time biometric ID MAY be used for prevention of a specific, substantial, and imminent threat to life or physical safety, or a genuine and present/foreseeable terrorist threat — strictly necessary use only

decomposed_atoms.description: Exception (iii): identification of suspects for serious crimes
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Exception: real-time biometric ID MAY be used for localization or identification of suspects of criminal offences punishable by custodial sentence of at least 4 years, for offences listed in Annex II — strictly necessary use only

decomposed_atoms.description: All exceptions require prior judicial authorization
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Use under any exception SHALL require prior judicial authorization — except in duly justified urgency, where authorization must be sought within 24 hours. If authorization is refused, use must cease immediately and all data deleted

decomposed_atoms.description: All exceptions must comply with proportionality and necessity requirements
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Use under any exception SHALL be subject to temporal, geographic, and personal limitations, fundamental rights impact assessment, notification to market surveillance authority, and registration in the EU database (Art. 49)

cross_references.context: Detailed conditions, safeguards, and procedures for the law enforcement biometric ID exceptions
EU database registration requirement for real-time biometric ID systems under exception
List of criminal offences for which Art. 5(1)(h)(iii) exception may apply
Penalties for Art. 5 violations: up to EUR 35M or 7% of global annual turnover

scope_notes.absolute_prohibitions_no_exceptions: ART05-PRO-1a, ART05-PRO-1b, ART05-PRO-1c, ART05-PRO-1e — absolute prohibitions with no exceptions (harm threshold still applies for OBL-1 and OBL-2)
scope_notes.prohibitions_with_narrow_exceptions: ART05-PRO-1d (human assessment + objective facts exception), ART05-PRO-1f (medical/safety exception), ART05-PRO-1g (dataset labelling + law enforcement exception), ART05-PRO-1h (three narrow law enforcement exceptions with judicial authorization)
scope_notes.enforcement_date: 2025-02-02 — Art. 5 prohibitions are in force since this date (6 months after entry into force of the AI Act)
scope_notes.penalty_tier: Highest under the AI Act: up to EUR 35,000,000 or 7% of total worldwide annual turnover (Art. 99(1))
